pages.raw_text:
The Electrical Workers union
had an inning last night at the meet.
ing of the Central Labor union when
they asked that the w. w. P. Co. be
placed on the fair list. The matter
came up through a communication
from the union requesting that the
power monopoly be placed on the
fair list. They stated that their &C
ton in accepting an open shop" and
returning to work was due to in
struction received from the Pacific
council of their organization which
recommended such course.


The communication occasioned
considerable discussion and the
electrical workers came in for some
criticism over the manner in which
they had involved the unions in a
fight with the local monopoly and
had been among the first to drop the
contest, leaving the other unions in
the lurch.


e. A. Cooney spoke on the ques-
ton at considerable length. He ad-
mitted that when the Central Union
had taken up the fight for the line
men it had done so as matter of
sympathy and that the union had
not inaugurated the fight according
to the laws of the Central body or
under conditions that were alto
gether fair to the company, but he
said the unions had realized what
any labor organization was up
against when seeking fair treatment
